analysis: MPC aggressiveness gated trial + evaluation hot-path profile

redoHistory.reason: confirmationMatch candidate run used exact_from_empties=18 (default) instead of 16 (baseline), confounding the paired comparison; work log/report/meta also mis-stated the value as 16

screening.method: proxy (T156b Gate1 style, held-out root NWS[-1,0) approximation), no new engine search
screening.criterion: agreementDrop<=0.02 and errorIncrease<=0.05, most aggressive (smallest) t among passing

confirmationMatch.openings: T175 P1 primary set, first 15 (same as baseline)
confirmationMatch.note: raw game-log JSON is gitignored (matches T175 precedent); only this report/meta and the aggregated statistics are committed

redo1Correction.issue: First run's vs_edax.py invocation omitted --engine-exact-from-empties 16, so candidate ran with the default (18) while baseline (T175 P1) used 16 -- a confound the verifier caught. The work log/report/meta also incorrectly stated '16' despite the actual run using 18.
redo1Correction.fix: Re-ran the 30-game confirmation with --engine-exact-from-empties 16 explicit. Old ef=18 raw results archived with an -ef18 suffix (gitignored, local only). Added a settings-match guard (validate_settings_match, with --self-test) to t176_confirmation_compare.py that raises if baseline/candidate key settings (depth, exact_from_empties, time_ms, max_nodes, quota, unlimited_exact_empties, tt_mb, weights, weightsSha256) differ.

anomalyExplanationCheck.conclusion: The confound explains most of the +26% node-count anomaly; after correction the live-game node metric is near parity (within noise), consistent with game-tree divergence being the dominant remaining source of variance (already flagged as an unreliable metric in this report; the controlled fixed-position experiment in controlledNodeComparison is the reliable measurement).

controlledNodeComparison.method: calibrate_mpc gate (new --mpc-margin-t flag), fixed position set, depth 12, off vs t=1.5 vs t=1.0
controlledNodeComparison.positions: t156_mpc_positions.json test split, empty-bucket 21-28 only (first 60 by --max-positions, other buckets out of time budget)

engineWiring.mpcRsChanges: Added Calibration.sigma_centidisc; added calibration_with_margin_t(base, t) helper
engineWiring.searchRsChanges: Added SearchCtx.mpc_margin_t: Option<f32> (default None); added search_with_eval_with_policy_and_margin_t as the new entry point; existing search_with_eval_with_policy now a thin None-passing wrapper
engineWiring.calibrateMpcChanges: Added --mpc-margin-t <f32> to `gate` subcommand (requires --mpc on); added GateConfig.mpc_margin_t_permille for audit/resume
engineWiring.vsEdaxPyChanges: Added --engine-mpc-margin-t <float>, threaded through engine_best/play_game, recorded in settings/run_key
defaultBehaviorUnchangedProof: eval_cli CLI smoke: --enable-mpc alone vs --enable-mpc --mpc-margin-t 1.5 produced identical output except elapsedMs/nps (wall-clock) and the echoed mpcMarginTOverridden flag

profile.method: throwaway bench (engine/tests/t176_score_profile_bench.rs, deleted after use, same convention as T168 npsReference)
profile.note: canonical table_index conversion is a single Option branch + array index (O(1) per pattern); reasoned to be cheap by code inspection, not separately isolated by measurement

appliedOptimization.change: cell_trit/pattern_state_index: hoist (own, opp) derivation from mover out of the per-cell loop to once per pattern (was recomputed via `match mover` on every cell)

bitIdenticalProof.method: git stash (patterns.rs only) + eval_cli eval --depth 0 on 1200 positions, compare full-output SHA-256 before/after

npsMeasurement.method: throwaway bench (engine/tests/t176_score_hotpath_bench.rs, deleted after use), search_with_eval depth 12 time_ms=None on a representative midgame position, 3 runs each
npsMeasurement.verdict: within measurement noise; ranges overlap (before max 892626 > after min 884350); likely already performed by LLVM's loop-invariant code motion in release builds

proposedNotApplied: incremental (differential) evaluation: recompute only patterns touched by the move's flipped cells instead of all 46 instances per call; output-changing risk + interaction with TT/ETC needs broader verification, deferred to the 7/26 Codex consultation or a separate task
memoization/caching of canonical table_index lookups if repeated (class_id, raw_state) pairs are shown to recur heavily in the search tree; requires separate measurement of recurrence rate first

testResults.ffoFast: 5/5 positions solved correctly (score matches expected), nodes=641077417, unchanged from prior baseline
